commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Queue a narrow lint repair for the four unnecessary assertions; maintainer security and compatibility acceptance remains a separate merge decision after the branch is green.; Needs attention: No supply-chain issue was found, but the PR intentionally changes a credential-bearing Gateway response and needs maintainer confirmation of the Talk secret-read boundary.; Review finding: [P3] Remove the unnecessary typ... (sha=e09546fc4852b21924dc726a82354ad1d4b73c33)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Gateway `talk.config` so authorized `includeSecrets` callers can receive the active Talk provider API key as a string in `talk.resolved.config.apiKey`, while source provider maps stay redacted/source-shaped and new regressions cover scoped and read-scope behavior.

PR surface: Source +88, Tests +325. Total +413 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection on current main shows `includeSecrets` returns before runtime SecretRef resolution, while iOS only accepts a string `apiKey`. I did not run a physical iOS session in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Queue a narrow lint repair for the four unnecessary assertions; maintainer security and compatibility acceptance remains a separate merge decision after the branch is green.

target.title: fix(gateway): iOS Talk treats SecretRef-backed API keys as missing
target.body:
Closes #98209
Related: #91020

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes iOS native Talk loading when the active Gateway Talk provider uses a SecretRef-backed `apiKey`. Before this change, scoped `talk.config({ includeSecrets: true })` could return the source-shaped SecretRef where iOS expects a resolved string, so the iOS app treated the provider as missing credentials.

## Why This Change Was Made

The Gateway now resolves the active Talk provider `apiKey` only after the existing `operator.talk.secrets` or admin scope check has passed, and exposes that materialized value only in `talk.resolved.config.apiKey` for the active provider.

The source-shaped config remains source-shaped: provider maps keep SecretRef-shaped keys, raw inline provider-map keys are redacted from the source payload, and sibling/realtime secret-like fields are not promoted into a new native-client credential surface.

## User Impact

Paired iOS clients with Talk secret scope can use SecretRef-backed native Talk provider configuration without copying a plaintext provider key into config or adding a separate local mobile override.

Clients without Talk secret scope continue to receive redacted config. Older Gateways still have the original SecretRef behavior, so the fix takes effect when the Gateway is updated.

## Evidence

Reproduction and live fix proof:

- Reproduced the failure path from current `main`: `includeSecrets: true` returned before runtime Talk provider resolution, leaving iOS without a string `talk.resolved.config.apiKey` for SecretRef-backed provider keys.
- Verified this branch against a live local Gateway WebSocket using an isolated temp config/state dir and fake env-backed SecretRefs.
- Verified read-scoped `includeSecrets` is rejected.
- Verified read-scoped normal config does not leak the fake credential marker.
- Verified scoped callers receive the active resolved provider key only at `talk.resolved.config.apiKey`.
- Verified source provider SecretRefs remain source-shaped, inactive raw provider keys are redacted, and sibling/realtime secret-like fields are redacted.

Focused checks:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/talk.test.ts src/gateway/server.talk-config.test.ts
```

Result: 92 tests passed.

```text
git diff --check
```

Result: clean.

```text
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local
```

Result: clean; no accepted/actionable findings.

Not tested: physical iOS app session and live ElevenLabs synthesis. The live Gateway proof used fake credential strings only; no real API key is included in this PR, tests, or commits.
target.author: ooiuuii

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
